why_discrepancy: Source age_num is integer years and was explicitly truncated for PHI per CIDC_Annotations. Template age is decimal years (e.g. 50.5 / 47.9). No source file in S1400I-clinical/ contains per-sample collection dates or non-truncated age. Critical finding: template age is per-PATIENT (single value across all timepoints for the same patient) not per-sample -- so collection dates would not help reproduce it.
example_cimac_part_ids: CCZRBHF (template age=50.5; source age_num=50; constant across Baseline/Cycle_2/Cycle_4/Cycle_5/Progression)
source_quote: 'age_num | age_num | Age truncated ... Age truncated to remove identifying specificity' / 'age_num | age_num | PHI removed ... changed age > 89 to 90 or older ... PHI' (S1400I-clinical/CIDC_Annotations_S1400I_20230323.xlsx Sheet1)
interpretation: Decimal portion was deliberately truncated upstream and cannot be recovered from any file in the repo. The earlier 'per-sample collection date' framing is incorrect: template age is per-patient decimal not per-sample. All 160 patients have a single age value across timepoints; diff (template - source) is in [0.0 0.9].
template_value_status: Source-supported upstream but not reproducible from current files
exact_human_decision_needed: Either (a) supply S1400I-clinical/age_decimal.csv (columns: cimac_part_id age_decimal) with per-patient un-truncated age from SWOG sponsor and wire into s1400i extractor; OR (b) accept enrollment-integer age (age_num) as a degraded substitute and document divergence; OR (c) accept S1400I age as permanently NA. A per-sample collection-date file is NOT the right ask.
